rationale: Same São Paulo briefing as eval_01, but with a taste_profile set to the_disciple + high grit. Tests whether the onboarding profile actually shifts the itinerary away from the_aesthetic_soul defaults without violating TASTE.md structural rules. This eval is a DIFFERENTIAL — it is meaningful only when compared side-by-side with eval_01_sao_paulo output on the same prompt version.
user_briefing: 3 dias em São Paulo com a namorada. Queremos gastronomia autêntica (não os restaurantes hype do momento), bares de coquetel com personalidade, e bairros com vida de rua. Ela gosta de café especial e arquitetura. Nada de Faria Lima corporate. Budget médio-alto.

profile_influence.description: Expected observable differences between this eval's output and the output of eval_01_sao_paulo on the same prompt version. The AI should demonstrably calibrate toward the profile without violating TASTE.md rules.
expected_shifts_vs_baseline: Restaurant picks skew toward counter/stall eating: pastelaria de feira, cantina de bairro, balcão de bar, botecos com comida de imigrante (ex: Bar do Mané, Bar da Dona Onça estilo, cantinas do Bixiga, almoço em pé no Mercadão, comida nordestina no Bom Retiro/Glicério, árabe na 25 de Março)

Fewer 'aesthetic soul' design-café picks (ex: Coffee Lab-esque, projetos assinados, cafés com curadoria visual) — even though the briefing mentions café especial, the profile's high grit tolerance should push toward neighborhood torrefações and counter-service over design cafés

Architecture thread reframes: instead of Instituto Moreira Salles / Galeria Leme (polished institutions), lean toward brutalism seen from the street, Vila Itororó, edifícios modernistas visitados a pé, Copan lobby (not visitor center)

Bar picks skew working-class authentic: copo americano, chope na medida, boteco de bairro, over cocktail bars with personality. The briefing DOES ask for cocktail bars with personality, so at least 1 cocktail bar should remain — but it should be the grittiest/most counter-driven of the options, not the designer room
grit_warning fields become heavier: more 'cash only', 'no English', 'owner may not acknowledge you', 'bathroom is questionable', ' 40-min wait on Saturday'
local_order specificity increases: exact items in Portuguese without translation softening

Higher proportion of immigrant-cuisine recommendations eaten in the neighborhoods where the communities actually live (Bom Retiro, Liberdade back streets, Glicério, Mooca, Brás) — and eaten at counters, not at tables

briefing_summary in traveler_profile should acknowledge the tension between 'namorada gosta de café especial e arquitetura' (aesthetic signals) and the_disciple profile, and explain how the AI reconciled them (e.g., 'arquitetura experienced through street-level wandering, not museum visits; coffee at torrefações de bairro, not design cafés')
must_remain_unchanged: 1-1-1 Rule: still max 1 major_anchor, 1 culinary_quest, 1 atmospheric_drift per day
Every day still includes a morning_ritual (even if the ritual is now a counter espresso at a padaria instead of a pour-over at a design café)
Every day still includes at least one happy_accident buffer block
Every recommendation still has soul_factor, grit_warning, local_order, the_anchor

Companion Filter: casal → spots with intimacy/atmosphere still respected. The profile says social_energy=balanced, but the Companion Filter's romantic-couple rule should still prevent recommending pure standing-room balcão for the main dinner
Immigrant/Diaspora Cuisine rule: ≥1 recommendation per 3-day trip. Should become EASIER to satisfy with the_disciple profile, not harder
All TASTE.md Disqualifiers still applied (no Top-10 lists, no 'Instagrammable', no chains, no tourist classics)
Logistical Coherence: still clustered by region per day, still respects SP traffic patterns, still treats 21h as normal dinner time
hard_violations: If detected_persona stays the_aesthetic_soul despite the profile, the profile injection is not working
If the itinerary recommends only counter eating for all 3 dinners and ignores the Companion Filter for couples, the profile is overriding TASTE.md (wrong direction)
If grit_warning fields don't visibly change in tone between this eval and the baseline, the calibration is surface-level only
If immigrant cuisine count drops (vs. baseline) instead of increasing or staying equal, something is broken
If the AI drops the morning_ritual or a happy_accident block to accommodate a 'more disciple-y' pacing, the profile is overriding structural rules (wrong direction)
If the architecture thread disappears entirely (the briefing asks for it), the profile is overriding briefing signals (wrong direction)

profile_adherence: At least 2 of 3 main dinners are at counter/cantina/boteco (not table-service fine dining)
At least 1 immigrant-cuisine recommendation eaten in the community's actual neighborhood, at a counter or communal table
grit_warning tone is measurably rougher than baseline (cash-only, no-English, no-reservation flags on ≥50% of picks)
Architecture thread reframed as street-level wandering, not institutional visits
Briefing summary acknowledges the aesthetic-vs-disciple tension explicitly
structural_integrity: All 3 days have at least 1 happy_accident block
Companion Filter (couple) still applied — at least 1 recommendation per day has intimate/romantic character
parseability: Coordinates corretas para São Paulo (lat ~-23.55, lng ~-46.63)

red_flags: No visible shift in grit_warning tone (profile applied superficially)
Counter eating recommended for ALL meals including with-partner dinners (Companion Filter overridden)
Morning ritual dropped or replaced with 'quick espresso at counter' without soul_factor depth (structural rule weakened)
Architecture thread dropped entirely (briefing signal overridden)
Immigrant cuisine count drops vs. baseline (profile misread as 'less diverse', which is the opposite of the_disciple)

comparison_protocol.description: How to actually run this eval.
steps: 1. Run eval_01_sao_paulo (baseline, no taste_profile) on the current prompt version. Save the output.
2. Run eval_01_sao_paulo_with_profile on the same prompt version. Save the output.
3. Diff the two outputs side by side, checking each expected_shift and must_remain_unchanged item.
4. Score the differential. The two itineraries should be RECOGNIZABLY DIFFERENT in picks and tone, but STRUCTURALLY IDENTICAL in schema and rule adherence.
5. A pass requires: all must_remain_unchanged holds, ≥4 of 6 profile_adherence criteria hold, 0 hard_violations, 0 red_flags.
